issues.title: Incorrect grouping
issues.problem: The query is grouping by merchant_id instead of customer_id.

issues.title: Missing filter for completed transactions
issues.problem: The query does not filter for completed transactions only.

issues.title: Missing table aliases
issues.problem: The query does not use table aliases, making it harder to read.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic.
issues.fix: /* Calculate total transaction amount per customer for completed transactions */

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT t.customer_id, SUM(t.amount) AS total_amount
FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions t
WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY t.customer_id
review.summary: The query had critical correctness issues and could benefit from readability improvements.

issues.title: Missing customer_id in GROUP BY
issues.problem: The query is missing customer_id in the GROUP BY clause, which will cause an error.

issues.title: Case Sensitivity
issues.problem: Table names should be consistent in case. SQL is case-insensitive in some environments, but it's best practice to keep naming consistent.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining its purpose and logic.
issues.fix: -- Calculate total transaction amount by merchant for completed transactions

review.corrected_sql:
-- Calculate total transaction amount by merchant and customer for completed transactions
SELECT customer_id, merchant_id, SUM(amount)
FROM fact_transactions
WHERE status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY customer_id, merchant_id;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with the GROUP BY clause and some readability issues. It has been corrected and improved.

issues.title: Missing customer_id in GROUP BY
issues.problem: The query is missing customer_id in the GROUP BY clause, which will cause incorrect aggregation.

issues.title: Case Sensitivity
issues.problem: Table names are in uppercase which is not consistent with typical SQL conventions.
issues.fix:
SELECT customer_id, merchant_id, SUM(amount)
FROM fact_transactions
WHERE status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY customer_id, merchant_id;

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT customer_id, merchant_id, SUM(amount)
FROM fact_transactions
WHERE status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY customer_id, merchant_id;
review.summary: The query had a correctness issue with missing customer_id in the GROUP BY clause and a readability issue with inconsistent case usage.

issues.title: Missing customer_id in GROUP BY
issues.problem: The query is missing customer_id in the GROUP BY clause, which will cause an aggregation error.

issues.title: Case sensitivity in table names
issues.problem: Table names should be consistently cased to avoid confusion and potential errors.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the purpose and logic, which can be helpful for future maintainers.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose of each clause.

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT customer_id, merchant_id, SUM(amount)
FROM fact_transactions
JOIN dim_merchant ON fact_transactions.merchant_id = dim_merchant.merchant_id
WHERE status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY customer_id, merchant_id;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with the GROUP BY clause and some readability issues. The corrected query includes the missing customer_id in the GROUP BY clause and adheres to consistent casing.
